---
title: "Mission control"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Mission control}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This page is the R-side mission-control board for the `hsquared` /
`HSquared.jl` twin project. It is static and repo-versioned. The source of
truth remains the tests, GitHub Actions, issue ledger, check log, and public
claims register.

```{=html}
<style>
.hs-mission {
  --ink: #172026;
  --muted: #5a6670;
  --line: #d7dde2;
  --panel: #f8fafb;
  --blue: #245b73;
  --green: #2f6f5e;
  --amber: #8b641d;
  --red: #9d3f3f;
  --violet: #5d568f;
  color: var(--ink);
}
.hs-mission * { box-sizing: border-box; }
.hs-hero {
  border: 1px solid var(--line);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 22px;
  margin: 16px 0 20px;
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f8fbfc 0%, #eef5f1 54%, #f7f7fb 100%);
}
.hs-kicker {
  margin: 0 0 8px;
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 0.86rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
}
.hs-hero h2 {
  margin: 0;
  font-size: 1.75rem;
  line-height: 1.12;
  letter-spacing: 0;
}
.hs-hero p {
  max-width: 78ch;
  margin: 10px 0 0;
  color: var(--muted);
}
.hs-grid {
  display: grid;
  gap: 12px;
}
.hs-grid.metrics {
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr));
  margin: 0 0 18px;
}
.hs-grid.two {
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr));
  margin-bottom: 14px;
}
.hs-card {
  border: 1px solid var(--line);
  border-radius: 8px;
  background: #fff;
  padding: 14px;
}
.hs-card h3 {
  margin: 0 0 8px;
  font-size: 1rem;
  letter-spacing: 0;
}
.hs-metric {
  min-height: 106px;
}
.hs-metric strong {
  display: block;
  font-size: 1.45rem;
  line-height: 1;
  margin-bottom: 7px;
}
.hs-metric span,
.hs-card p,
.hs-card li {
  color: var(--muted);
}
.hs-tagrow {
  display: flex;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  gap: 6px;
  margin-top: 10px;
}
.hs-tag {
  display: inline-flex;
  align-items: center;
  border: 1px solid var(--line);
  border-radius: 999px;
  padding: 3px 8px;
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  background: var(--panel);
}
.hs-tag.done {
  border-color: #a7c8bb;
  color: var(--green);
  background: #eff8f5;
}
.hs-tag.partial {
  border-color: #d9c491;
  color: var(--amber);
  background: #fff8e8;
}
.hs-tag.plan {
  border-color: #c5c3dd;
  color: var(--violet);
  background: #f5f4fb;
}
.hs-tag.block {
  border-color: #dfb3b3;
  color: var(--red);
  background: #fff1f1;
}
.hs-road {
  display: grid;
  gap: 8px;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.hs-phase {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: minmax(78px, 112px) 1fr;
  gap: 10px;
  padding: 10px;
  border: 1px solid var(--line);
  border-radius: 8px;
  background: var(--panel);
}
.hs-phase b {
  color: var(--blue);
}
.hs-phase span {
  color: var(--muted);
}
.hs-table {
  width: 100%;
  border-collapse: collapse;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.hs-table th,
.hs-table td {
  border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line);
  padding: 8px 6px;
  text-align: left;
  vertical-align: top;
}
.hs-table th {
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 0.82rem;
}
.hs-list {
  margin: 8px 0 0;
  padding-left: 18px;
}
.hs-lenses {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr));
  gap: 8px;
  margin-top: 8px;
}
.hs-lens {
  border: 1px solid var(--line);
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 9px;
  background: var(--panel);
}
.hs-lens b { display: block; }
.hs-lens span {
  color: var(--muted);
  font-size: 0.88rem;
}
@media (max-width: 620px) {
  .hs-hero { padding: 16px; }
  .hs-hero h2 { font-size: 1.35rem; }
  .hs-phase { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
}
</style>
<div class="hs-mission">
  <section class="hs-hero" aria-labelledby="hs-mission-title">
    <p class="hs-kicker">hsquared / HSquared.jl mission control</p>
    <h2 id="hs-mission-title">One easy R interface, one Julia engine, one evidence gate.</h2>
    <p>
      This board tracks the R lane and its contract with the Julia twin. It
      separates implemented package surfaces, experimental validation paths,
      and roadmap capabilities that are deliberately still planned.
    </p>
    <div class="hs-tagrow" aria-label="current status tags">
      <span class="hs-tag done">Phase 0 public scaffold complete</span>
      <span class="hs-tag done">v0.1 Gaussian animal model fits by default (REML)</span>
      <span class="hs-tag partial">Phase 1 validation and comparators active</span>
      <span class="hs-tag block">Multivariate, genomic, GPU still planned</span>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="hs-grid metrics" aria-label="top metrics">
    <article class="hs-card hs-metric">
      <strong>1</strong>
      <span>public formula contract parsed today: <code>animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped)</code></span>
    </article>
    <article class="hs-card hs-metric">
      <strong>20</strong>
      <span>validation-status rows separating covered, partial, and planned evidence</span>
    </article>
    <article class="hs-card hs-metric">
      <strong>1</strong>
      <span>model fitted by default: the v0.1 univariate Gaussian animal model (REML via the HSquared.jl engine)</span>
    </article>
    <article class="hs-card hs-metric">
      <strong>0</strong>
      <span>production GPU, QTL/eQTL, or GLLVM capabilities (planned)</span>
    </article>
  </section>

  <section class="hs-grid two" aria-label="lane status">
    <article class="hs-card">
      <h3>R Interface Lane</h3>
      <p>Current focus: make the first animal-model contract obvious, tested, and honest.</p>
      <ul class="hs-list">
        <li>Implemented: default fit of the v0.1 Gaussian animal model (REML via the HSquared.jl engine), package scaffold, pkgdown, parser diagnostics, data-container diagnostics, fitted-object extractors.</li>
        <li>Experimental: opt-in local Julia bridge targets for supplied-variance Henderson MME checks and the REML-only sparse optimizer.</li>
        <li>Planned: multivariate/genomic production fitting, QTL/eQTL, GLLVMs, unusual inheritance, ML estimation, and real backend execution.</li>
      </ul>
    </article>
    <article class="hs-card">
      <h3>Julia Twin Boundary</h3>
      <p><code>HSquared.jl</code> owns engine utilities and performance work. R owns the applied user language.</p>
      <ul class="hs-list">
        <li>Bridge payload: <code>y</code>, <code>X</code>, sparse <code>Z</code>, method, family, encoded IDs, and pedigree metadata.</li>
        <li>Result contract: compact fields that R can turn into summaries, extractors, and diagnostics.</li>
        <li>Rule: no public R claim moves from planned to working until the Julia path and R bridge are both validated.</li>
      </ul>
    </article>
  </section>

  <section class="hs-card" aria-label="phase board">
    <h3>Phase Board</h3>
    <div class="hs-road">
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 0</b><span>Operating system, public memory, pkgdown, GitHub Actions, claim gates.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 1</b><span>Simple Gaussian animal model: parser, Ainv, likelihood, MME, EBVs, h2, validation fixtures.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 2</b><span>Repeatability, permanent environment, maternal/paternal effects, common environment, dominance, custom kernels.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 3</b><span>Multivariate Gaussian animal models, G/R/P matrices, genetic correlations, missing trait records.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 4</b><span>Factor-analytic G matrices with <code>diag()</code>, <code>lowrank(K)</code>, and <code>fa(K)</code> covariance structures.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 5</b><span>GBLUP, SNP-BLUP, single-step, APY, marker effects, QTL/GWAS/eQTL scans.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 6</b><span>GLLVM-style high-dimensional animal models, non-Gaussian responses, omics, ordination.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 7</b><span>Selfing, clonal, haplodiploid, polyploid, cytoplasmic, imprinting, epistasis, unusual inheritance.</span></div>
      <div class="hs-phase"><b>Phase 8</b><span>Accelerator-aware and HPC scaling: CPU default, optional GPU backends, checkpoints, benchmarks.</span></div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="hs-grid two" aria-label="evidence and gates">
    <article class="hs-card">
      <h3>Evidence Now</h3>
      <table class="hs-table">
        <thead><tr><th>Surface</th><th>Status</th><th>Evidence</th></tr></thead>
        <tbody>
          <tr><td>Formula parser</td><td><span class="hs-tag partial">partial</span></td><td><code>animal(1 | id, pedigree = ped)</code> and <code>hs_data()</code> pedigree shorthand parse and validate.</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Bridge payload</td><td><span class="hs-tag partial">partial</span></td><td>Tests pin <code>y</code>, <code>X</code>, sparse <code>Z</code>, IDs, method, family, pedigree metadata.</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Validation atoms</td><td><span class="hs-tag partial">partial</span></td><td>Tiny Ainv, Mrode9/nadiv Ainv comparator, Henderson MME, sparse REML identity, and Mrode-style supplied-variance output checks.</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Fit object</td><td><span class="hs-tag partial">partial</span></td><td>Extractor contract exists for objects that contain matching result fields.</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Data container</td><td><span class="hs-tag partial">partial</span></td><td><code>hs_data()</code> checks phenotype, pedigree, genotype, marker, expression, annotation, and environment metadata.</td></tr>
          <tr><td>Backends</td><td><span class="hs-tag plan">planned</span></td><td><code>hs_control()</code> stores planned names; <code>backend_info()</code> marks execution unavailable.</td></tr>
        </tbody>
      </table>
    </article>
    <article class="hs-card">
      <h3>Blocked Claims</h3>
      <ul class="hs-list">
        <li>No fitting beyond the v0.1 univariate Gaussian animal model (the default fits that model; multivariate, genomic, factor-analytic, and non-Gaussian models remain planned).</li>
        <li>No standard errors / confidence intervals for variance components or h2 (out of v0.1 scope).</li>
        <li>No ASReml/BLUPF90-family multi-package parity or large-scale (&gt;10k) production-fitting claim; the v0.1 fit is validated to gryphon scale (~1300 animals) and on clean simulated pedigrees.</li>
        <li>No genomic prediction, marker scan, QTL/eQTL, GLLVM, or unusual-inheritance fitting claim.</li>
        <li>No CPU/GPU execution, backend benchmark, or speedup claim.</li>
      </ul>
    </article>
  </section>

  <section class="hs-card" aria-label="review lenses">
    <h3>Review Lenses</h3>
    <p>These are review perspectives, not automatically running agents.</p>
    <div class="hs-lenses">
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Ada</b><span>programme integration</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Shannon</b><span>lane coordination</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Boole</b><span>formula grammar</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Noether</b><span>math consistency</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Gauss</b><span>numerical engine</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Fisher</b><span>inference and estimands</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Curie</b><span>validation canon</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Pat</b><span>applied user reading</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Rose</b><span>public claim audit</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Grace</b><span>CI and release gates</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Hopper</b><span>R-Julia bridge</span></div>
      <div class="hs-lens"><b>Karpinski</b><span>Julia performance</span></div>
    </div>
  </section>
</div>
```

## Recovery Pointers

Before a substantial resumed slice, run the rehydrate commands in `AGENTS.md`,
then read:

- `docs/dev-log/coordination-board.md`;
- `docs/dev-log/check-log.md`;
- newest `docs/dev-log/after-task/*.md`;
- `docs/design/01-v0.1-contract.md`;
- `docs/design/06-public-claims-register.md`.

The short rule is still the one in the project memory:

```text
Private memory routes us.
Repository memory governs us.
Live repo state verifies us.
```
